notes: Illinois state biometric privacy and AI laws. Illinois BIPA is the strongest biometric privacy law in the US with a private right of action.

data.description: Illinois law regulating the collection, use, storage, and destruction of biometric identifiers and information.
data.notes: Effective 2008. The most significant US biometric privacy law due to its private right of action. Requires informed written consent before collection of biometric identifiers (fingerprints, retina/iris scans, voiceprints, hand/face geometry). Mandates retention schedule and destruction guidelines. Prohibits sale or profit from biometric data. Private right of action with statutory damages: $1,000 per negligent violation, $5,000 per intentional/reckless violation. Has generated extensive litigation, particularly against tech companies and employers using biometric time clocks.
